name: Mehen
description: Mehen is an ancient Egyptian serpent-deity who protected the sun god Ra during his nightly journey through the underworld, Duat. Depicted as a giant serpent coiled around Ra's barque, Mehen shielded him from the dangers lurking in the darkness. The name 'Mehen' translates to 'coiled one,' perfectly capturing the serpent's form and protective role. Mehen's coils provided a safe haven for Ra and other deities, allowing them to navigate the treacherous waters of the underworld and emerge safely at dawn. The serpent was not only a protector but also a symbol of cyclical renewal and rebirth, reflecting the daily cycle of the sun's journey. Its presence in funerary texts and tomb decorations emphasizes its connection to the afterlife and the deceased's journey to eternal life. Mehen's image served as a powerful amulet, offering protection and guidance to those facing the perils of the unknown, both in life and after death. The game of Mehen, played on a board shaped like a coiled serpent, may have been a ritualistic reenactment of Ra's journey through the underworld, with the players taking on the roles of deities and demons. The game's outcome could have been seen as influencing the success of Ra's nightly voyage and the subsequent return of the sun.
subtitle: The Coiled Protector of Ra
appearance: Mehen is depicted as a gigantic serpent, its body coiled into a spiral shape. The scales are often a vibrant green or blue, symbolizing life and rebirth. Its eyes are piercing and watchful, constantly vigilant for threats. The serpent's head is adorned with protective symbols, such as the uraeus (cobra) or the sun disk, further emphasizing its divine status. The size of Mehen is immense, capable of encircling Ra's barque multiple times, providing a formidable barrier against the forces of darkness.
habitat: Mehen primarily resides in the Duat, the Egyptian underworld, specifically around the barque of Ra during its nightly voyage. Its presence is also felt in temples and tombs, where its image is invoked for protection and guidance in the afterlife. Mehen's influence extends to any space where protection from darkness and chaos is needed.
behavior: Mehen is a vigilant and protective deity, constantly alert to the dangers lurking in the underworld. Its primary purpose is to safeguard Ra from the attacks of Apep and other malevolent forces. Mehen is also seen as a guide, helping souls navigate the treacherous paths of the afterlife. It is a patient and enduring being, embodying the cyclical nature of time and the eternal struggle between light and darkness.

keyMyths.title: Ra's Nightly Journey
keyMyths.description: Mehen protects Ra as he travels through the Duat each night, battling Apep and other forces of chaos. Its coils provide a safe haven for Ra, allowing him to emerge victorious at dawn. This ensures the continuation of the world and the cycle of life.

sources.author: E.A. Wallis Budge
sources.description: A comprehensive translation and analysis of the ancient Egyptian funerary text.
sources.text: A comprehensive translation and analysis of the ancient Egyptian funerary text.

sources.author: Richard H. Wilkinson
